title: pleopeltis collinsii
intro: Pleopeltis collinsii, also known as the Collins' polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its creeping rhizome and small, lanceolate leaves. Traditionally, the Collins' polypody has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis collinsii are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the leaves and rhizome of Pleopeltis collinsii are typically dried and infused in hot water to create a tea, or used in tinctures and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
